title: Protocol for the Production of Crude Alcoholic Extracts from Native Plants
author: Marissol M Leite
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9vch62w

steps.title: Separate the leaves and inflorescences from...
steps.instruction: Separate the leaves and inflorescences from plants with the help of scissors.

steps.title: Rinse the material under running water...
steps.instruction: Rinse the material under running water and then with distilled water to remove debris.

steps.title: Dry the material with paper towels...
steps.instruction: Dry the material with paper towels to remove the excess of water.

steps.title: Grind the leaves and flowers with...
steps.instruction: Grind the leaves and flowers with a knife mill or blender and / or just cut with the help of scissors, in order to allow a better extraction of secondary compounds.

steps.title: Weight the vegetable mass into an...
steps.instruction: Weight the vegetable mass into an analytical balance and dilute in 70% alcohol.

steps.title: After quantifying the vegetable mass, dilute...
steps.instruction: After quantifying the vegetable mass, dilute and mash the crushed and / or cut material into 70% alcohol in the desired ratio, such as 1:1 (1 gram of vegetal material to 1 milliliter of 70% alcohol) until the thoroughly solution mix.

steps.title: After maceration and dilution in 70%...
steps.instruction: After maceration and dilution in 70% alcohol filter the obtained solution with the aid of filter paper in a beaker.

steps.title: Identify the alcoholic crude extract and...
steps.instruction: Identify the alcoholic crude extract and store in an amber glass in the refrigerator
